BIOGRAPHY.text: Sun Xiushun is a former sailor who established a presence in Singapore's shipping sector in 2006. He developed a bauxite trade corridor between China, Singapore, and Indonesia before pivoting to mining and infrastructure in Guinea following Indonesian export restrictions in 2013. He co-founded the SMB-Winning consortium in 2014 to develop upstream bauxite operations.

INTERESTING_FACTS.text: He began his career as a sailor. His transition from shipping into major mining operations in West Africa was a survival-driven pivot triggered by regulatory changes in Indonesia.

GIVING.text: Through Winning International Group and its consortiums, he has funded community infrastructure in Guinea, including two hospitals and local roads. He has also established training and scholarship programs and has expressed a commitment to building more schools and hospitals to improve healthcare and education access.

There is no mention of personal philanthropy, but under his leadership, the SMB-Winning Consortium has engaged in CSR initiatives such as donating 1,540 solar panels, organizing dental health campaigns, and providing training for Guinean employees in China.

POTENTIAL_CONNECTORS.text: Key business and project partners include China Baowu Steel Group, Rio Tinto, Chalco Iron Ore Holdings, and the government of Guinea.
Frédéric Bouzigues (Managing Director of SMB); Winning Shipping Ltd.; United Mining Supply; Shandong Weiqiao Group; China Yantai Port Group.

Alpha Condé (President of Guinea), Mr. Magassouba (Minister of Mines and Geology), Mr. Curtis (Minister of Investment and Public-Private Partnerships), Mr. Wazni (Chairman of SMB), and Frédéric Bouzigues (SMB CEO)

KEY_POSITIONS.text: Founder and Chairman of Winning International Group.
Chief Executive of SMB-Winning; Leader of the SMB-Winning Consortium.
President and CEO of the SMB-Winning Consortium
Founder and president of Winning International Group

ADVERSE_NEWS.text: While no personal adverse news is mentioned, the SMB-Winning Consortium he leads has faced significant criticism and controversy regarding environmental degradation, dust pollution, and the pollution of water resources for local communities in Guinea.
